title: podman run: systemstack called from unexpected goroutine
body:
New flake seen in cron, [sys f33 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/4643688180613120/logs/main.log) which, I assume, we don't exactly care about:
```
[+0655s] not ok 155 podman selinux: containers in --no-infra pods do not share context
         # (from function `die' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 363,
         #  from function `run_podman' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 181,
         #  in test file test/system/410-selinux.bats, line 162)
         #   `run_podman run --rm --pod myselinuxpod $IMAGE cat -v /proc/self/attr/current' failed with status 133
         # $ podman rm --all --force
         # $ podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
         # $ podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
         # quay.io/libpod/testimage:20200929 766ff5a3a7e4
         # $ podman pod create --name myselinuxpod --infra=false
         # 736ab926874162623ca500c25d1440a7aab6757047bc45c4315c6effd0bd6f7f
         # $ podman run --rm --pod myselinuxpod quay.io/libpod/testimage:20200929 cat -v /proc/self/attr/current
         # fatal: systemstack called from unexpected goroutine
         # [ rc=133 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
Looking through my flake logs, I see a small number of instances, in both `sys` and `int` tests, but nothing since January 2022. I'm filing this and will close it immediately: the sole purpose is for me to have a reference if/when I see it again in cron jobs on obsolete systems.
